figures.caption: Figure 1: CoCoT-EEG. A. Raw EEG signals are processed through a bank of 1D convolutions with varying kernel sizes (k = 3 to 121). The resulting multiscale feature maps are patchified into temporal tokens via L2 pooling, followed by the addition of a Fourier positional encoding. B. Self-attention is applied to the tokens to produce EEG embeddings (z). A residual MLP head with two GLU blocks is used for the downstream classification or regression. C. For pretraining, a MoCo-style contrastive learning framework [22] is employed, where query xquery and key xkey samples are generated via EEG augmentations. The encoder and momentum encoder produce embeddings (zq and zk+), which are trained with InfoNCE [23] against a queue of negative samples zki . GLU: gated linear unit; MH: multi-head, MoCo: momentum contrast; N: number of transformer blocks; PE: positional encoding.

Table 1: Performance of CoCoT-EEG trained from scratch, or pretrained (PT) with MoCo on TUEG and finetuned. Balanced accuracy (in %) is reported for all tasks except SEED-VIG (Pearson corr.). Baseline performances are taken from published values in [10] [5]. Mean ± 95% CI is reported for all models except REVE (mean ± std). Bold denotes SOTA mean. FT: end-to-end fine-tuning. ⋆ denotes runs using generalization finetuning techniques such as Mixup or Euclidean-Alignment [39]

Table 2: Pretraining objective and architecture ablations. Comparing CoCoT-EEG’s backbone with all kernels with MoCo (base) vs. MAE pretraining, as well as against Convolution-first and Patch-first variants with truncated convolution kernels. Pearson correlation for SEED-VIG, balanced accuracy (%) for the rest. Values are mean ± 95% CI. Bold denotes the best result per row.

Table 4: Effect of individual augmentations across datasets (balanced accuracy, %). “Leave-one-out” reports the difference between the full augmentation setup vs. without that augmentation. “Leaveone-in” reports the difference between from scratch and pretraining with only that augmentation. Positive values indicate improved performance. Bold denotes the best per column.

Table 5: Linear Probing (LP) vs. end-to-end finetuning (FT) across pretraining setups (Pearson correlation for SEED-VIG, balanced accuracy in % for the rest, mean ± 95% CI). ∆ denotes gain from LP to FT. Bold denotes the best FT result per row.
Table 6: Pretraining hyperparameters for TUEG and HBN.
Table 7: Finetuning hyperparameters for TUEG and HBN.
Table 8: Overview of downstream task datasets.

Table 10: Performance comparison including additional baselines from secondary sources. Balanced accuracy is displayed in % (mean ± 95% CI where available). Baseline performance is highlighted in light gray.

Table 11: Performance of CoCoT-EEG trained from scratch, or pretrained (PT) with MoCo on TUEG or HBN and finetuned on each dataset. Balanced accuracy (in %, mean ± 95% CI) is reported for all tasks except SEED-VIG (Pearson correlation). LaBraM [7], CBraMod: mean ± 95% CI [8], REVE: mean ± s.t.d. [10], CSBrain: mean ± s.t.d. [5]. Bold denotes SOTA mean. FT: end-to-end fine-tuning.
